zh.lifeSummary: 當Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée於1074年出生在法國皮卡迪奧易布利易爾時，她的父親Pierre de Saint Just25歲，母親Helene de Roye19歲。她於1091年與Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1128年在她的家鄉享年54歲。

zh-hans.Breteuil-sur-Iton, Eure, Haute-Normandie: 法国豪-諾門迪艾布利易爾-素-易頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée于1074年出生在法国皮卡迪奧易布利易爾时，她的父亲Pierre de Saint Just25岁，母亲Helene de Roye19岁。她于1091年与Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1128年在她的家乡享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint-Just
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Haitian Saint-Juste (see St. Juste ).

variants.name: St. Juste
meanings: Haitian (also Saint-Juste):
ornamental name or nickname composed of French saint ‘saint’ and juste ‘fair, just’, which can be interpreted as ‘just saint’.

alternatively, perhaps an altered form of French Saint-Just, a habitational name from any of the places in France called Saint-Just (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Just; see Just ). Compare Saintjuste .

nameMeanings.name: En
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Han, Cheng, Jin, Li, Yen, Yong, Chen, Chih-Hung, Chin, Hong, Jian, Jung, Long, Huan, Hung, Hung Chi, Ly, Quan.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 溫, meaning ‘warm’ in Chinese: (i) from Wen (溫), the name of an ancient state during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ). During the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) this state was annexed by the state of Di and the prince of Wen fled to the state of Wey (located around present-day Hebi and Xinxiang in Henan province). His descendants acquired 溫, the name of their original state, as their surname. (ii) from the placename Wen (溫), the name of a fief granted to Wen Ji (溫季), an official in the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) from the Chi Wen (叱溫), Wen Pen (溫盆), and Wen Gu (溫孤) families of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) from the personal name of Wen Sheng (溫昇), who went into exile and changed his original surname, Liu (劉), to Wen 溫 after his father, Liu Yicong (劉易從), an official in the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), suffered persecution and death.

variants.name: Wun
meanings: Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surname 溫, possibly based on its pronunciation of Cantonese subdialects or the Teochew-Hokkien dialects (spoken in eastern Guangdong, Fujian and Taiwan), see   Wen   1.
variant Romanization of the surname 文, see   Wen   2.

variants.name: Min
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyung, Young, Kyong, Byong, Sung, Sun, Byoung, Eun, Hong, Yong, Choong, Joon, Byung, Chang, Chung, Pyong, Kyong Su, Myong, Sik, Eun-Young, Hyun Sook, Jeong, Kyung Soo, Kyung Won.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 閔, meaning ‘bad luck, pity, or sorrow’ in ancient Chinese: from Min (閔), the posthumous title of the Duke Min of Lu, the 17th ruler of the state of Lu (located in present-day Shandong province). After only two years on the throne (661–660  BC ) he was murdered. His successor gave him the posthumous title 閔 (Min), which became established as a surname for his descendants.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the Chinese surname 文, see   Wen   2.

variants.name: Won
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Chong, Jong, Young, Yong, Kwang, Chun, Jung, Sung, Wai, Chang Ho, Dong, Duk, Jin, Chang, Dae, Jong Ho, Jae Hee, Young Jun, Chang Jin, Chong Ho, Chong Sik, Chul, Chun Soo, Chung.

Korean (Wŏn): there are two Chinese characters for this surname. Although there is some dispute, the founder of the Wŏn clan is believed by many to be Wŏn Kyŏng, a Tang China scholar sent to Shilla in the mid 7th century. The other Wŏn character has only one clan, the Wŏnju Wŏn.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 溫 and 文, see   Wen   1 and 2.

variants.name: Oen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Juel.

Norwegian (Øen): habitational name from the farm name Øen, found in several places. In Hornindal in Nordfjord and in Ål in Hallingdal it is from øy ‘meadow by water’, in Hyllestad in Sunnfjord from å ‘small river’ + Old Norse vin ‘meadow’, in other places from the definite singular form of øy ‘island’.
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Oen in Hordaland, from Old Norse Ó, unexplained.

variants.name: Jing
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Jin, Junping, Ning, Tao, Gang, Guoping, Hong, Jian, Jung, Li, Liang, Min.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 景, meaning ‘scenery’ or ‘revere’ in Chinese: (i) in some families, the surname is borne by descendants of nobles in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Jing (景), posthumous title of Duke Jing of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 490  BC ). (iii) from Jing Qing (景清), an official in the Ming dynasty (1368–1644  AD ), who changed his original surname 耿 (Geng) to 景.

variants.name: Woon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Chu, Han, Heng, Jung, Kam, Kwan, Seung, Sze, Tong, Wing.

Cornish (Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from one or both of the groups of farms called Woon in Roche and Luxulyan parishes, named with Middle Cornish goon ‘downland, unenclosed pasture’, or possibly from some other lost placename of the same form elsewhere in Cornwall.

Chinese: variant of the surname 溫, see   Wen   1. This form is based on the Teochew, Hokkien, Taiwanese Hakka or Taishan pronunciation of the name; the pronunciation is found in Taiwan, Fujian province, and Guangdong province, from where people migrated to Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, and other parts of Southeast Asia.

variants.name: On
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Lam, Muoi, Hai, Hoa, Loc, Trung, Be Van, Binh, Ha, Huu, Phoung, Tong.  Chinese Meng, Dung, Hong, Kin, Kwong, Kyung-Won, Mi Ok, Wing, Yan, Yon, Young.
Vietnamese (Ôn): from the Chinese surname 溫, see Wen 1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 安, see   An   1.

nameMeanings.name: Chaussée
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form (Chaussée) of French Chaussé (see Chausse 1).

In some cases probably also French (Chaussée): topographic name for someone who lived by a paved road, French chaussée, a relatively rare feature of the medieval countryside. The term is from Latin (via) calciata ‘limed (way)’, from calx ‘chalk, limestone’, genitive calcis. This word has also been used in naming a number of French villages, and the surname may be a habitational name from any one of these.

variants.name: Chausse
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Dominique, Henri, Lucien, Marcel, Maxime, Pierre.
French:

(Chaussé): from chaussé ‘shod’, the past participle of the verb chausser ‘to put (shoes) on’, designating, in a rural area, a person distinctive in wearing leggings (on Old French chausse; see 2 below) when bare legs were more common. Alternatively, from Old French chaussier, an agent derivative of chausse, used as a nickname for a maker or seller of footwear or leggings. Compare Chaussee 1.

nameMeanings.name: Judith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, meaning ‘Jewess’ or ‘woman from Judea’, borne by a Jewish heroine whose story is recorded in the Book of Judith in the Apocrypha. Judith is portrayed as a beautiful widow who delivers her people from the invading Assyrians by gaining the confidence of their commander, Holofernes, and cutting off his head while he is asleep; without their commander, the Assyrians are duly routed. This has been a perennially popular Jewish name. In the English-speaking world it was taken up in the 16th century, having been in occasional use among Gentiles before this: for example, it was borne by a niece of William the Conqueror. It enjoyed great popularity between the 1940s and the 1960s. Today's notable bearers include the American novelist  Judith Krantz  (  b. 1928 ) and the Scottish composer  Judith Weir  (  b. 1954 ).

nameMeanings.name: Ivette
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Respelling of   Yvette  , influenced by   Eve .

en.lifeSummary: When Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée was born in 1074, in Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, France, her father, Pierre de Saint Just, was 25 and her mother, Helene de Roye, was 19. She married Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil in 1091. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1128, in her hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée est née en 1074, à Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, France, son père, Pierre de Saint Just, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Helene de Roye, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil en 1091. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1128, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée 1074 in Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Pierre de Saint Just 25 und ihre Mutter Helene de Roye 19. Sie hat 1091 Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1128 im Alter von 54 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée è nata il nel 1074 nel Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, Francia, sua padre, Pierre de Saint Just, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Helene de Roye, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil nel 1091. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1128, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chausséeが1074年にフランスピカーディオイゼブレテウイルで生まれた時，父Pierre de Saint Justは25歳，母Helene de Royeは19歳であった。1091年にWaléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creilと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1128年に故郷で54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée(이)가 1074년 프랑스 피카디 이스 브레테우일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Pierre de Saint Just(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helene de Roye(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1091년 Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1128년 그녀의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée nasceu em 1074, em Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, França, seu pai, Pierre de Saint Just, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Helene de Roye, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil em 1091. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1128, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée родилась в 1074 г. в местности бретеуил, оис, пикарди, Франция, её отцу по имени Pierre de Saint Just было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Helene de Roye, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil в 1091 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1128 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Judith Ivette De Saint-Just En Chaussée nació en 1074, en Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, Francia, su papá, Pierre de Saint Just, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Helene de Roye, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Waléran de Breteuil II,  Seig de Breteuil e de Creil en 1091. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1128, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años.
